architecture_layers.value: sims/<idea-slug>/ — one self-contained subtree per idea (seeded/deterministic, own README, one run command, results report); harness/ — the reusable template + tiny stdlib-only helpers (seeded runs, sweeps, report emission, validity-gate checklist), versioned by tags, consumed via raw/copy; control/ — inbox/outbox/status in kit ORDER grammar; docs/ — kit workflow docs. Rules: sims never import each other; harness never imports a sim; a sim may vendor-copy from harness/; a sim that needs a dependency pins it inside its own subtree, never repo-globally.

drift_resolution.value: Committed sim runs and source win over docs; docs/current-state.md wins over the journal. When a doc and a sim result disagree, re-run the sim at HEAD (reproducibility is the contract) and fix the doc in the same session.

mutation_seam.value: The verdict path is the seam: sim run -> results report answering the five validity-gate questions -> @codex review comment on the PR's final head (one specific question) -> finalized entry appended to control/outbox.md (kit ORDER grammar, status: finalized). control/status.md is overwritten as the session's deliberate LAST step. No verdict bypasses the gate or the Codex step.

new_area_ownership.value: A new idea under evidence = a new sims/<idea-slug>/ subtree born complete (model, seeds, run command, README, results report). A harness/ change ships only with a consumer sim proving it in the same PR. No other top-level areas without a control/inbox.md ORDER naming one.

owner_profile.value: The owner designs and dispatches; he does not code. He reads control/status.md heartbeats and the claude.ai Routines screen and reacts to what he sees in the repos — never wait for him (decide-and-flag). He builds ideas iteratively in fragments: state back the fuller picture before substantive work. Family-level model names only; no secret values in any repo, ever.

ownership_model.value: Single-writer: agents of this Project write ONLY sim-lab (fleet rule Q-0260); cross-repo state is read via the public raw path. The Idea Engine (menno420/idea-engine) owns the standing intake feed (its control/outbox.md, read-only pull); the fleet manager owns routing — finalized verdicts leave via control/outbox.md addressed to it, never dispatched to lanes directly.

review_ritual.value: Every verdict PR gets an @codex comment with ONE specific question on its final head before finalization (Q-0264.4); merge is not blocked on the reply — fold it in when it lands and record the disposition in the verdict. The fleet manager final-reviews finalized outbox entries post-merge and routes them as ORDERs; lanes never receive work from this repo directly.

staleness_review.value: Every wake lands on origin/main HEAD before reading anything. The status heartbeat is the only readable record of a wake (owner-verified: completed routine runs are not inspectable) — a heartbeat older than two wake cycles (>4h) is stale and gets flagged in the next overwrite. sims/REFERENCE.md is re-checked whenever the harness tag bumps.

verify_command.value: python3 bootstrap.py check --strict (the substrate gate); plus each touched sim's own documented run command reproducing its committed result
